title: Ensure that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments are set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments are set as appropriate
input_row.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
input_row.rationale: API server communication contains sensitive parameters that should remain encrypted in transit. Configure the API server to serve only HTTPS traffic. Impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments exist and they are set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection on the apiserver. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the TLS certificate and private key file parameters. --tls-cert-file=<path/to/tls-certificate-file> --tls-private-key-file=<path/to/tls-key-file> Default Value: By default, --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file are presented and created for use. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. http://rootsquash.com/2016/05/10/securing-the-kubernetes-api/ 3. https://github.com/kelseyhightower/docker-kubernetes-tls-guide
input_row.impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_tls_cert_and_key_file_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification, typically located in the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` (for clusters using static Pods), or via the Kubernetes API if the control plane is managed as a Pod.
- Use `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` to retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod spec, or directly inspect the manifest file if running outside Kubernetes management.
- Parse the `command` or `args` section of the kube-apiserver container to verify the presence of the `--tls-cert-file` and `--tls-private-key-file` flags.
- Validate that both flags are present and reference non-empty file paths.
- Optionally, verify that the referenced files are mounted into the container via the `volumeMounts` and `volumes` sections.
- Pass Criteria: Both `--tls-cert-file` and `--tls-private-key-file` arguments are set with valid, non-empty paths in the kube-apiserver configuration.
- Fail Criteria: Either argument is missing, empty, or not set in the kube-apiserver configuration.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
